text: Reranking re-scores an initial candidate set with a cross-encoder so the most relevant passages rise to the top before they reach the model. It adds latency but lifts precision at small k.

Hybrid retrieval fuses dense vector similarity with sparse keyword matching, then combines the two ranked lists, usually with reciprocal rank fusion. It catches both paraphrase matches and exact-term matches.
Chunk size and overlap shape what a retriever can find. Chunks that are too large dilute the embedding; chunks that are too small lose the surrounding context a question needs.
A vector database stores embeddings and answers approximate-nearest-neighbour queries. HNSW indexes trade a little recall for large speed gains over a brute-force scan.

GraphRAG extracts entities and relationships at ingestion and traverses the resulting knowledge graph at query time. It pays off when answers depend on connections between entities, not similarity alone.
The Model Context Protocol exposes each external system as a server that an agent calls through one standard interface, so a failing tool returns a typed error instead of cascading through the app.
An orchestrator-workers pattern has one coordinating agent decompose a task and hand sub-tasks to specialised workers, then synthesise their outputs into a final answer.

Long contexts degrade: as you stuff more tokens into the window, models lose information in the middle and reasoning quality drops. Dosing the model with only what the step needs works better than filling the prompt.
An evaluation harness scores a system against a fixed set of cases so a change can be measured instead of guessed. Without one, teams ship prompt edits on vibes.
Tracing records each step an agent takes — the prompt, the tool call, the result — so a failure can be replayed and debugged instead of disappearing into a single opaque response.
An LLM gateway puts one provider-agnostic endpoint in front of every model. It centralises keys, retries, caching, spend tracking, and failover, so swapping models is a config change.
Embeddings map text to vectors whose distances approximate meaning. Smaller embedding models are cheaper and faster but separate near-duplicates less cleanly than larger ones.
Agentic retrieval lets the model decide whether to search, what to search for, and when it has enough to answer, looping retrieve-evaluate-refine instead of retrieving once.
Prompt caching reuses the encoded prefix of a stable system prompt across calls, cutting cost and latency when many requests share a long, unchanging preamble.
Guardrails sit around a model to block prompt injection, strip personal data, and enforce output schemas. They are a boundary, not a feature you bolt on after launch.
Runaway agent loops happen when a model keeps calling tools without converging. A step cap and a stop condition bound the cost so one bad task can't drain a budget.
Fine-tuning adapts a base model to a narrow task or style by training on examples. It is rarely the first move; retrieval and prompting usually close the gap at lower cost.
Structured outputs constrain a model to emit JSON that matches a schema, so downstream code can parse it reliably instead of scraping prose for fields.
Tool-call approval pauses an agent before a risky action and asks a human to confirm. It keeps irreversible operations like refunds or deletes under human control.
Cost in an agent system grows with the number of model calls, not just tokens per call. A ten-step loop is ten invocations, and the bill follows the step count.
Durable workflow steps persist progress so a long task survives a timeout or crash. Completed steps are not re-run when the workflow resumes.

Recall measures how many of the relevant passages a retriever actually returned; precision measures how many of the returned passages were relevant. Reranking trades recall breadth for top-of-list precision.
Data residency rules require some workloads to keep data inside a region or on specific infrastructure. Architecture, not policy text, is where residency is actually enforced.
Session memory holds the working state of a single run; durable memory holds facts retrieved across runs. Mixing the two without tiers is how context windows fill with noise.
